title: Image Generation
description: Developer generates images from text prompts using image-capable providers (OpenAI DALL-E, XAI, etc.).

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Initiates image generation requests via the Image facade or PendingImageGeneration fluent API

actors.name: AI Provider
actors.description: External image generation service (OpenAI DALL-E, XAI Aurora). Implements ImageProvider + ImageGateway interfaces.

actors.name: Queue System
actors.description: Laravel queue that executes GenerateImage jobs asynchronously

use_cases.name: Generate Image Synchronously
flow: Developer optionally chains: size(), quality(), attachments(), timeout()
Developer calls generate(provider?, model?) on PendingImageGeneration
PendingImageGeneration resolves provider list via Provider::formatProviderAndModelList()
Iterates providers; calls Ai::fakeableImageProvider() to resolve concrete provider
Provider::image() dispatches GeneratingImage event, then delegates to imageGateway()->generateImage()
ImageGateway (e.g. XaiImageGateway) POSTs to AI Provider API
Returns ImageResponse containing GeneratedImage collection, Usage, Meta
use_cases.failover: On FailoverableException, fires ProviderFailedOver event and tries next provider in list

use_cases.name: Queue Image Generation
flow: Validates attachments are StoredImage or LocalImage (not transient)
Dispatches GenerateImage job onto Laravel queue
Returns QueuedImageResponse wrapping the pending dispatch

use_cases.name: Configure Image Options
options: attachments(array<Image>): reference images sent with request
use_cases.note: All return $this for chaining. Included in UC1 and UC2.

use_cases.name: Select Provider and Model
use_cases.description: Developer passes a provider identifier (string, array, or Lab enum) and optional model name to generate() or queue(). Falls back to config('ai.default_for_images') if null.

use_cases.name: Fake Image Generation (Testing)
flow: Developer calls Image::fake(responses) → registers FakeImageGateway via Ai::fakeImages()
generate() calls record the ImagePrompt via Ai::recordImageGeneration()
FakeImageGateway::generateImage() returns a pre-configured or auto-generated ImageResponse
preventStrayImages() can throw if unexpected generation occurs

use_cases.name: Assert Image Generated
use_cases.note: Testing assertions. Delegates to Ai::assertImageGenerated() etc. Requires UC5 fake to be active.

use_cases.name: Assert Image Queued
use_cases.note: Testing assertions for queued generations. Delegates to Ai::assertImageQueued() etc.

key_relationships: PendingImageGeneration depends_on GenerateImage job (for queue path)
Provider (concrete) uses_trait HasImageGateway — holds ImageGateway reference
ImageProvider (contract) interface requires imageGateway(), useImageGateway(), defaultImageModel(), defaultImageOptions()
ImageGateway (contract) interface requires generateImage(ImageProvider, model, prompt, ...): ImageResponse
XaiImageGateway implements ImageGateway — POSTs to xAI /images/generations, returns b64_json as GeneratedImage
FakeImageGateway implements ImageGateway — returns fake/pre-configured ImageResponse without HTTP call
GenerateImage (job) aggregates PendingImageGeneration and re-calls generate() on execution
GeneratesImages trait fires GeneratingImage and ImageGenerated events around gateway call

key_classes.name: ImageProvider
methods: image(prompt, attachments, size, quality, model, timeout): ImageResponse

key_classes.name: ImageGateway (contract)
methods: generateImage(ImageProvider, model, prompt, attachments, size, quality, timeout): ImageResponse

key_classes.name: FakeImageGateway
key_classes.note: For testing; returns pre-set or auto-faked ImageResponse

key_classes.name: GeneratesImages
key_classes.note: Implements ImageProvider.image(); fires GeneratingImage/ImageGenerated events; delegates to imageGateway()->generateImage()

key_classes.name: HasImageGateway
key_classes.note: Stores $imageGateway; falls back to $this->gateway if not set

response_objects: QueuedImageResponse: wraps PendingDispatch from GenerateImage job
important_notes: Provider failover: if a provider throws FailoverableException, ProviderFailedOver event is fired and the next provider in the list is tried
Queue path validates attachments must be StoredImage or LocalImage (serialisable) — raw Image objects not allowed
Fake mode: Ai::fakeableImageProvider() swaps in FakeImageGateway transparently; generation is recorded for assertions
XAI gateway ignores attachments/size/quality params (passes only model+prompt to API)
Events fired: GeneratingImage (before), ImageGenerated (after), ProviderFailedOver (on failover)
